diary: Started the day right with coffee and a run around Green Lake—the water was glass-smooth this morning, and I needed that quiet before the shift. Got to Station 1 by 8:30, ran through equipment checks like always. Around 10, a bunch of us headed down to Coliseum Bar & Grill to catch the USA-Wales match. The place was packed already, everyone in a good mood with the weather so nice. Grabbed a food truck lunch after, some decent tacos near Pioneer Square. The afternoon was steady—drills, maintenance, nothing major came through, which I'll take. Cooked dinner with the crew, just some pasta and vegetables. By evening I was feeling that familiar 24-hour tiredness creeping in. Got home around 5:30, showered off the day, and now I'm ready to sleep for a solid stretch.
thought_process: The World Cup match pulled me away from station duties for a couple hours, but honestly, I think the crew needed that mental break. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive to First Hill anyway. It was a smooth, uneventful shift—no major calls—which meant more time for the routine maintenance and training I usually rush through. The weather lifted everyone's mood, including mine. I've been doing this job long enough to know that good weather and good morale go hand in hand, and today felt like one of those Monday gifts where nothing goes wrong and you leave feeling intact. I'm looking forward to proper rest.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
